Popular searches Faith in the North Duncan Lomax/Ravage Productions Breadcrumb Home About the Archbishop Back About the Archbishop Stephen Cottrell is the 98th Archbishop of York. Serving the Diocese of York and the dioceses of the Northern Province of the Church of England. Meet Stephen Cottrell Discover more about the 98th Archbishop of York, The Most Reverend Stephen Cottrell. The Archbishop’s team The Archbishop is supported by a dedicated team of advisers, administrators and clerical, technical and maintenance staff at... Previous Archbishops of York Stephen Cottrell is the 98th Archbishop of York to have served the church in the Northern Province. Ministry and role of the Archbishop The Northern Province The provinces of Canterbury and York reflect the beginnings of the structure of the Church of England The Anglican Communion The Archbishop is one of the Primates of the Anglican Communion representing the Church of England internationally. Diocese of York An overview of the Archbishop's work and ministry in the Diocese of York. York Minster York Minster is the seat of the Archbishop of York and the cathedral church of the Diocese and Province of York. Patronages Archbishop Stephen is patron to a number of charitable organisations, locally, nationally and internationally. Explore further Publications Stephen Cottrell has written more than 20 books on subjects such as evangelism, spirituality, and discipleship, as well as... Prayer for the Day Sunday, 3 May 2026 The Fifth Sunday of Easter Your browser does not support the audio element. Risen Christ, your wounds declare your love for the world and the wonder of your risen life: give us compassion and courage to risk ourselves for those we serve, to the glory of God the Father. Amen.
